groups.text: It is currently 20 degrees and cloudy in Berlin-Tempelhof.
With the morning weather set, here is the news.

NVIDIA is pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence by moving beyond chatbots and into the physical world. At the CVPR conference, the company unveiled new agent skills designed to accelerate the development of robotics, vision AI, and autonomous vehicles. This shift toward Physical AI is supported by the launch of NVIDIA NemoClaw, which enables self-evolving agents to synthesize research more securely. At the same time, the open-source community is challenging proprietary ecosystems. Nous Research has released Hermes Desktop, an open-source AI agent application under the MIT license, aiming for cross-platform availability.

These technological leaps in automation coincide with a volatile period for global trade and security. US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer maintains that the United States and the European Union remain committed to their trade deal, even as the US proposes new tariffs. In other diplomatic shifts, Bangladesh's Khalilur Rahman has been elected President of the 81st UN General Assembly.

The geopolitical tension is most acute in the Middle East, where a reported Iranian attack on Kuwait has left one person dead and several others injured. This escalation follows a period of intense repression within Iran and a broader conflict involving the US and Israel.

This instability in global security is mirroring a decline in Germany's own international and economic standing. For the first time, Germany has failed to secure a seat on the UN Security Council, a blow to its diplomatic prestige that has already sparked political friction between the Greens and the CDU.

The diplomatic setback is compounded by a sharp drop in foreign investment. US pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly has halved its planned multi-billion dollar investment in a German production site. The company explicitly cited the German government's austerity measures and spending cuts as the reason for the pullback.
After the headlines, back to the weather.
This afternoon will be cloudy with temperatures between 22 and 23 degrees and rain chances up to 23 percent.
And from the weather, a quick look at the calendar.

On your calendar today: all day, 'Department Retro', at 10:00, 'Optikrunde', at 10:30, 25 minute 'Research weekly', at Gauss; note: -::~:~::~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~::~:~::- Join with Google Meet: Or dial: (DE) +49 40 8081616038 PIN: 8..., at 11:00, 'MxM Spotlight 🔦', note: Dear Innovators, You're invited to MxM Spotlight, our weekly knowledge-sharing platform! MxM Spotlight brings together the best of our previous PM, T..., at 15:00, 'Corsnero OffGPT', note: Corsnero OffGPT, at 15:00, 2 hour 'Gruppenmeeting Gregor', and at 15:00, 2 hour '🧠☕ Research Seminar', note: -::~:~::~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~:~::~:~::- Join with Google Meet: Or dial: (DE) +49 40 8081616571 PIN: 2....
And that's it for your personal morning broadcast.
